what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 presentation-style slide contrasting current fragmented practice with a unified one-space approach using two large cards, visual media panels, bullet lists, and a central connector.
observations: The page uses a centered fixed-size .view canvas with a subtle blueprint-grid decoration and footer texture.
The main content is two large side-by-side cards labeled Current practice and Our approach.
The left media panel is populated at runtime with tilted tile elements to suggest fragmented information.
The right media panel uses layered CSS gradients to suggest a calm unified surface.
A dashed connector with a circular plug icon sits between the two cards to imply movement from current state to solution.
The file relies on external Google Fonts and a local /surface/styles/vars.css token sheet.
standouts: The composition uses large visual zones rather than dense text.
The current-state visual is procedurally generated from a fixed seed array.
The connector is a distinct visual element separate from the cards.
conventions: Single-file HTML document with embedded CSS and a small inline script.
Uses design tokens through CSS custom properties such as --dna-space-72 and --gold.
Uses semantic-light div/span structure oriented around slide composition rather than document semantics.
Uses utility-like class names for visual roles such as .cWarm, .cCool, .clutter, and .calm.
concerns: The layout is fixed to 1600x900 and is not responsive within this file.
The script defines W and H constants that are not used.
The visual result depends heavily on CSS variables from /surface/styles/vars.css being present.
The inline script assumes the #clutter element exists before it runs.
notes: A self-contained slide visual for explaining a transition from fragmented current practice to a unified one-space solution.
questions: Is the 1600x900 fixed canvas intentional for export or presentation capture only?
Should the clutter tile dimensions track the actual media panel size instead of fixed pixel coordinates?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it shows current-to-solution movement through two big zones and a central connector.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make the solution feel like consolidation: scattered accounts, files, and channels become one controlled space with clearer communication and lower cost.
novelty: The file combines a deterministic runtime-generated clutter illustration with a calm gradient solution panel and an explicit plug-style transition marker.
contribution: A reusable current-to-solution slide pattern with two large comparison zones, generated fragmentation imagery, a unified-surface visual, and a centered movement connector.
summary_for_embedding: Fixed-size HTML slide showing fragmented current practice transitioning into a unified one-space approach through side-by-side cards, generated clutter tiles, gradients, bullets, and a connector icon.
intention_for_embedding: Designed to communicate a workflow transformation from scattered information and channels into centralized access, control, and communication.
